perspectives.excerpt: This week’s $28 billion US stock-market listing for SK Hynix Inc. may be the biggest-ever first-time share sale by a foreign company, but it isn’t just about raising cash. It’s also about competing in the hottest corner of the global stock market — memory chips used in AI computing.…

perspectives.headline: South Korea's SK Hynix, Samsung to Put AI Trade to the Test | The Pulse 7/6/2026
perspectives.excerpt: South Korea’s memory giants will put the artificial-intelligence trade to yet another test this week. Samsung releases earnings on Tuesday. Investors are also getting ready for SK Hynix’s massive US listing days later.…

stories.headline_en: What we know about Prince Harry's visit to the U.K. amid security dispute

perspectives.headline: What we know about Prince Harry's visit to the U.K. amid security dispute
perspectives.excerpt: What we know about Prince Harry's visit to the U.K. amid security dispute Prince Harry is arriving in London on Monday for a series of events linked to the Invictus Games. He was planning to bring Meghan and their two children for the first time in four years, but the BBC reports he is no longer doing that over a security dispute. Leigh Kiniry reports.

perspectives.headline: Prince Harry’s U.K. trip sparks fresh dispute over Buckingham Palace stay
perspectives.excerpt: LONDON — Britain’s Prince Harry will likely not be staying at the monarch’s longtime official residence when he visits London later this week, his spokesman said on Monday, in the latest twist in the his turbulent relationship with his royal relations.…

stories.headline_en: Micron signs long-term memory chip supply deal with Ford

perspectives.headline: Micron signs long-term memory chip supply deal with Ford
perspectives.excerpt: Micron Technology and Ford Motor signed a long-term supply agreement on Monday under which Micron will increase output of automotive memory chips to support Ford's next generation of vehicles. Securing a reliable pipeline of memory and storage components for Ford's vehicle production is the central aim of the deal.…

perspectives.headline: Micron and Ford signed a long-term deal to lock in memory chip supply for next-gen vehicles
perspectives.excerpt: Micron will expand production capacity to support Ford's vehicle programs as automakers compete for tightening chip supply

stories.headline_en: Chip Stocks Rally in AI Trade Revival After Plunge: Markets Wrap

perspectives.headline: Chip Stocks Rally in AI Trade Revival After Plunge: Markets Wrap
perspectives.excerpt: A rebound in chipmakers lifted stocks as dip buyers emerged amid speculation that the artificial-intelligence trade that has powered the bull market has more room to run. The dollar rose. Oil steadied.

perspectives.headline: Tech Stocks Rebound as Dip Buyers Pounce on Chip Pullback
perspectives.excerpt: US stocks climbed on Monday as investors jumped on technology shares after the blistering artificial-intelligence trade wavered heading into the holiday weekend.

stories.headline_en: Critical hearing for man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk

perspectives.headline: Critical hearing for man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk
perspectives.excerpt: Critical hearing for man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk Prosecutors on Monday aim to convince a judge at a key hearing that they have enough evidence against Tyler Robinson to go to trial and seek the death penalty. Robinson is accused of murdering conservative activist Charlie Kirk, whose widow and parents are expected to be in the courtroom. Carter Evans reports.

perspectives.headline: Prosecutors to present evidence against Charlie Kirk's alleged assassin
perspectives.excerpt: Prosecutors to present evidence against Charlie Kirk's alleged assassin, Tyler Robinson Prosecutors said they will seek the death penalty against Tyler Robinson. A crucial court hearing is expected to get underway on Monday to determine whether prosecutors have enough evidence to warrant a murder trial for the alleged assassin of conservative activist Charlie Kirk.…

stories.headline_en: Microsoft is selling off four Xbox studios as part of significant gaming cuts

perspectives.headline: Microsoft is selling off four Xbox studios as part of significant gaming cuts
perspectives.excerpt: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees today, and more than 30 percent of the job losses are in the company’s Xbox division. The significant gaming cuts will affect nearly every part of Xbox and also involve four game studios being spun off to be run independently from Microsoft.…

perspectives.headline: Microsoft's Xbox cuts 1,600 employees, with plans to shed 20% of its workforce this year
perspectives.excerpt: Microsoft's Xbox division plans to cut 20% of its workforce this fiscal year, according to a memo new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma sent to employees on Monday. Microsoft announced plans to lay off around 4,800 employees, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday, confirming Business Insider's earlier report.…

perspectives.headline: Australian PM apologises for 'inappropriate' comments about Kylie Minogue
perspectives.excerpt: Australian PM apologises for 'inappropriate' comments about Kylie Minogue - Published Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised "unequivocally" for comments he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.…

perspectives.headline: Australia’s PM Apologizes for ‘Inappropriate’ Comment About Kylie Minogue
perspectives.excerpt: Australia’s Prime Minister Anthony Albanese apologized “unequivocally” on Monday for insinuating in a recent podcast that he would “shag”—a vulgar term that means to have sex with—pop star Kylie Minogue.…

stories.headline_en: Teen describes seaplane's rough landing in NYC's East River: "I thought we were going to drown"

perspectives.headline: Teen describes seaplane's rough landing in NYC's East River: "I thought we were going to drown"
perspectives.excerpt: Teen describes seaplane's rough landing in NYC's East River: "I thought we were going to drown" The FAA is investigating after a hard landing by a seaplane that splashed down in the East River off Midtown Manhattan. Eight people were on board, including a 16-year-old who described the terrifying moment.

perspectives.headline: WATCH:  Passengers speak out after sea plane makes hard landing in East River in NYC
perspectives.excerpt: Passengers speak out after sea plane makes hard landing in East River in NYC A Kodiak 100 seaplane with eight people on board made a hard landing into the East River near Brooklyn, New York.…

perspectives.headline: SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell to donate stock to Trump Accounts
perspectives.excerpt: SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell announced she would donate to the Trump Accounts program on Monday, joining a growing list of companies and billionaires pledging to support the investment accounts for American children under 18.…

perspectives.headline: Elon Musk's No. 2 says she's giving SpaceX stock to Trump Accounts
perspectives.excerpt: SpaceX president Gwynne Shotwell says she's donating company stock to Trump Accounts, days after President Donald Trump said he expected the rocket maker's shares would find their way into the children's savings program.…

stories.headline_en: Physical media is dying. So is the idea that you own what you buy

perspectives.headline: Physical media is dying. So is the idea that you own what you buy
perspectives.excerpt: It’s been a long time coming. Physical editions of console games have steadily lost ground to digital versions, and more than one in four PlayStation 5 consoles sold in the United States since launch are now digital-only models, up from 18% in October 2024. Sony has seen where the market is headed.…

perspectives.headline: Sony says it will still make physical discs after 2028, as long as the game came out before then
perspectives.excerpt: Sony says it will still make physical discs after 2028, as long as the game came out before then Anything released after 2028 will still be digital-only. Sony's move to abandon physical game discs may have elicited the "Everyone disliked that" response from the gaming world, but there's a small consolation for game developers.…

stories.headline_en: Xbox To Lay Off 3,200 Workers, And Five Studios Will Leave The Brand

perspectives.headline: Xbox To Lay Off 3,200 Workers, And Five Studios Will Leave The Brand
perspectives.excerpt: The industry has been bracing for large-scale Xbox layoffs in the wake of a new administration running the brand. Today, those layoffs have arrived, and they are some of the largest in video game industry history. Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has announced that Microsoft will lay off around 3,200 workers over the next fiscal year, with 1,600 roles being affected today alone.…

perspectives.headline: Xbox confirms plans to lay off 3,200 workers over the next year
perspectives.excerpt: Xbox confirms plans to lay off 3,200 workers over the next year Half of the job cuts are taking place immediately while four studios are moving to new ownership. As expected, Microsoft is gutting its Xbox teams once again.…

stories.headline_en: OPEC+ Emerges From War to Threat of Oil Surplus

perspectives.headline: OPEC+ Emerges From War to Threat of Oil Surplus
perspectives.excerpt: Key members have agreed on another production hike, yet there may be little need for the extra barrels.

perspectives.headline: OPEC+ is raising oil output for a fifth straight month as Strait of Hormuz shipping recovers
perspectives.excerpt: Seven core members agreed to add 188,000 barrels per day in August, continuing a gradual unwinding of 2023 production cuts

stories.headline_en: Gold Falls as Traders Assess Fed Rate Outlook Ahead of Minutes

perspectives.headline: Gold Falls as Traders Assess Fed Rate Outlook Ahead of Minutes
perspectives.excerpt: Gold dropped as traders weighed the outlook for the Federal Reserve’s interest rate path at the start of a week in which the minutes of the US central bank’s last meeting are expected to shed light on policymakers’ thinking.

perspectives.headline: Chip stocks are trying to bounce back. The Dow is near 53,000. Fed minutes are coming
perspectives.excerpt: Federal Reserve minutes from Kevin Warsh's inaugural meeting as the central bank's chair will be released this week

stories.headline_en: Cargo ship reports being attacked in the Red Sea, maritime agency says

perspectives.headline: Cargo ship reports being attacked in the Red Sea, maritime agency says
perspectives.excerpt: "Vessels are advised to transit with caution and report any suspicious activity to UKMTO," the agency said. A cargo ship reported coming under attack in the Red Sea on Sunday, a British maritime agency announced.…

perspectives.headline: Cargo vessel in Red Sea reports coming under attack, UK maritime body says
perspectives.excerpt: A cargo vessel in the Red Sea has reported coming under attack, the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations Centre, a British maritime security alert service, said Sunday, amid a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran. The body said it received a report of the incident 30 nautical miles (56 km) southwest of the port city of Al Hudaydah in Yemen.…

stories.headline_en: Oscar Health’s Mark Bertolini on making ‘healthcare work for all’

perspectives.headline: Oscar Health’s Mark Bertolini on making ‘healthcare work for all’
perspectives.excerpt: Hello and welcome to  Modern CEO ! I’m Stephanie Mehta, CEO and chief content officer of Mansueto Ventures. Each week this newsletter explores inclusive approaches to leadership drawn from conversations with executives and entrepreneurs, and from the pages of  Inc.  and  Fast Company .…

perspectives.headline: Mark Bertolini Led Aetna and Bridgewater and Chairs the Verizon Board. What’s He Doing Running Oscar Health?
perspectives.excerpt: A veteran of big companies brings his leadership philosophy to a challenger brand.

stories.headline_en: Broadcom, Apple Extend Tie-Up to 2031 With New Custom Chips

perspectives.headline: Broadcom, Apple Extend Tie-Up to 2031 With New Custom Chips
perspectives.excerpt: Broadcom Inc. said that it will provide new custom chips to Apple Inc. in an expanded partnership that now runs through 2031.

perspectives.headline: Apple and Broadcom are extending their custom chip partnership through 2031
perspectives.excerpt: The two companies signed new multi-year agreements for Broadcom to develop and supply custom ASIC chips across multiple generations of Apple products
